evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor catches broad Exception and re-wraps as IOException — acceptable for I/O layer initialization but swallows the original type information (e.g., a NullPointerException from BlackLab startup would become an IOException).

findCollocations, executeDependencyPattern, executeDependencyPatternWithPos all guard against null/empty lemma by returning Collections.emptyList() silently — no logging, no warning. Callers have no visibility into whether an empty result reflects no matches or a skipped query.

buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution throws IllegalArgumentException for patterns not starting with '['. This is an unchecked exception from a private method. The public methods that invoke it (findCollocations) declare throws IOException in their signature but not IllegalArgumentException — callers who rely only on checked-exception contracts could be surprised.
CollocateQueryHelper.executeCollocateSearch wraps InvalidQuery as IOException('BCQL parse error') — consistent with executeCqlQuery which does the same wrapping. Good.

CollocateQueryHelper.scoreHits calls getTotalFrequency per collocate inside a loop; if getTotalFrequency throws, the exception propagates out of scoreHits, abandoning all results collected so far without any partial-result signal to the caller.

ExplorationHandlers catches IOException and IllegalArgumentException separately, sending 500 and 400 respectively. SketchHandlers.handleRelationQueryForPattern also catches IOException and sends 500. Pattern is consistent across handlers.

WordSketchApiServer.wrapHandler catches IOException (500) and Exception (500 'Unexpected error') — the generic Exception catch is appropriate as a last-resort fence but the error message does not include the handler name for the generic case.

strengths: Checked exception propagation via IOException is consistently used across the query layer.
InvalidQuery is consistently wrapped as IOException at each parser invocation.
Handler-level error dispatch (IOException→500, IllegalArgumentException→400) is applied symmetrically across ExplorationHandlers and SketchHandlers.
error_consistency.issue_character: The inconsistency is at the boundary between checked and unchecked exceptions: null/empty inputs are silently swallowed (empty return without logging), and an unchecked IllegalArgumentException can escape from public methods whose signature only declares IOException.
error_consistency.score_rationale: The codebase has a clear and consistently applied pattern for I/O error wrapping and HTTP status mapping. The deductions come from: silent empty-return guards that produce invisible failures, the IllegalArgumentException escape from buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution breaking the checked-exception contract, and partial-result abandonment in scoreHits without logging. These are real inconsistencies that affect operational diagnosability. Score of 73 reflects solid structural consistency with notable gaps at the null-guard and unchecked-exception boundaries.

issues.summary: Null/empty lemma guards return empty list silently without logging, making query skips invisible
evidence: findCollocations: if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { return Collections.emptyList(); } — no log
executeDependencyPattern: same pattern — no log, no signal to the caller
issues.suggestion: Add a logger.debug or logger.warn call before returning empty, e.g. logger.debug('findCollocations called with null/empty lemma, returning empty'); this preserves the guard behavior while giving operators visibility.

issues.summary: buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution throws unchecked IllegalArgumentException but callers only declare throws IOException
evidence: private static String buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution(...) { throw new IllegalArgumentException(...); }
findCollocations declares 'throws IOException' only; callers of findCollocations relying on the checked-exception contract will not catch IllegalArgumentException
issues.suggestion: Either convert the throw to IOException('Unrecognized CQL pattern format: ...', null) to keep it in the checked contract, or document the IllegalArgumentException in each public method's @throws Javadoc so callers know to handle it.

issues.summary: CollocateQueryHelper.scoreHits abandons all accumulated results if getTotalFrequency throws mid-loop
evidence: scoreHits iterates records and calls getTotalFrequency(collocateLemma) per record; if getTotalFrequency throws, the IOException propagates and the caller receives no partial results and no indication of how many records were processed
issues.suggestion: Wrap the getTotalFrequency call in a try/catch inside the loop, log a warning for the failed collocate, and continue processing remaining records. Return whatever partial results were accumulated.

root_causes: The silent null-guard pattern and unchecked exception escape both stem from a lack of a uniform policy for 'what happens when input is invalid' — some cases throw, some return empty, some log.
